data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"The Sea Peoples invasions were the primary military-political cause of simultaneous collapse, overwhelming palace centers by 1177 BC while Egypt survived due to superior defensive geography and institutional resilience","mechanism":"concatenation of maritime raiding and migratory incursions disrupted trade networks and destroyed administrative centers, while Egyptian defensive depth and Nilotic geography absorbed shocks","scope":{"covers":["Mycenaean Greece","Hittite Anatolia","Ugarit","Levantine city-states","Egyptian survival"],"excludes":["Minoan collapse","Mesopotamian continuity","post-collapse Dark Age cultural developments"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"unequivocal evidence that Sea Peoples arrived only after collapse had already occurred at all major palace centers","eval":"rubric:std-hist"},{"case":"archaeological demonstration that Egyptian military frontiers were breached and destroyed contemporaneously with Hittite and Mycenaean centers","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Emphasizes exogenous shock; Egypt as exception due to geography and institutional adaptability (Shoshenq I Libyan consolidation); faces criticism for being monocausal and potentially reversing cause-effect (Sea Peoples as symptom not cause)"}
